what_it_does: This file attaches a window.MS_SHAPES registry that maps brand entities and composition-kind categories to geometric shapes and returns CSS clip-path strings for polygon-based shapes.
observations: It is an IIFE bound to window.MS_SHAPES.
CLIP holds polygon() definitions for hexagon, octagon, diamond, triangle, and pentagon.
circle and rounded shapes are handled outside clip-path by returning 'none'.
ENTITY maps portal/wizard/hub/vi/generic to shape names and labels.
CATEGORY maps composition kind families (surface, component, style, motion, guidance, input, media, logic) to shape names.
Unknown ids, shapes, or categories log console.error and fall back to generic/rounded.
Internal lookup tables are exposed via _clip, _entity, and _category.
standouts: It acts as a single declarative source for entity and category shape mappings.
Composition kind families are deliberately mapped to diverse shapes so a catalog reads as a shape system rather than a text list.
It is loud on misuse, logging errors for unknown shapes, entities, or categories.
conventions: Uses an IIFE module pattern attached to the global window object.
Defensive fallback to a generic/rounded default on lookup failure.
Comments document the brand semantics of each shape mapping.
concerns: console.error calls may be undesirable in production.
Internal lookup tables are exposed, allowing external mutation.
'rounded' is not represented in CLIP and relies on unstated border-radius styling elsewhere.
Some shapes (triangle, pentagon) are defined but not referenced in ENTITY or CATEGORY.
notes: This file is the brand shape registry: it maps entities and composition categories to geometric shapes and supplies the matching CSS clip-path geometry.
questions: Where is the returned clipPath actually applied in the UI?
How is the 'rounded' shape styled (border-radius) in consuming components?
Are triangle and pentagon reserved for future entity/category mappings?
What data model defines the 'Composition KIND families'?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose matches the implementation: it is a single source for entity/category shape mapping and clip-path geometry.
apparent_themes: Visual identity consistency through shape semantics.
intention_signals: It appears designed to centralize shape semantics so that brand entities and composition categories are rendered consistently across platforms, using geometry as a recognizable shorthand.
novelty: It maps abstract composition kind families (surface, component, style, motion, guidance, input, media, logic) to distinct geometric shapes, turning a category catalog into a visual shape system.
contribution: It contributes a centralized shape registry and clip-path provider that a fused system would keep to maintain consistent shape rendering and to make entity/category shape assignments easy to extend.
summary_for_embedding: shapes.js is a window-attached IIFE registry that maps brand entities and composition kind categories to geometric shapes and returns CSS clip-path polygons for non-circular shapes.
intention_for_embedding: It was intended to be the single source of shape semantics and clip-path geometry for consistent cross-platform rendering of ConceptV entities and composition categories.

symbols.does: Returns the CSS clip-path string for a named polygon shape, or 'none' for circle/rounded and unknown shapes.
symbols.description: The clip function looks up a shape name in the CLIP table and returns the corresponding polygon() value; it treats 'circle' and 'rounded' as non-clipped shapes and logs an error while returning 'none' for unrecognized names.

symbols.does: Returns the shape, label, and clip-path for a brand entity id.
symbols.description: The entity function resolves an entity id against the ENTITY table, merges the id into the result, and attaches the computed clipPath via clip(); it falls back to the generic entity and logs an error for unknown ids.

symbols.does: Returns the shape name assigned to a composition kind category.
symbols.description: The forCategory function maps a composition kind category to a shape name using the CATEGORY table, falling back to 'rounded' and logging an error when the category is not recognized.

symbols.does: Returns the list of all available polygon shape names plus circle and rounded.
symbols.description: The shapes function enumerates the keys of the CLIP lookup and appends 'circle' and 'rounded' to produce the full set of shape identifiers supported by the registry.
